text: 100g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. everything else here is pretty flat. not relevant here.
0g of protein. the rest of the section is nothing special. also not relevant here.
vitamin e at 1% daily value, vitamin k at 1% daily value, vitamin a at 0% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side: strong cooking-stability case because tests whether practical cooking arguments can rescue brutal fat chemistry. simple ingredient identity because cleaner than industrial spreads. strong healthy-food halo because likely audience argument magnet.

on the weaker side: saturated fat is extreme because this should hammer it hard if the category means what it says. overall fat profile is weak because very poor fit for an elite fats ranking. health reputation can outrun the data because useful editorial counterweight.
C tier. Best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
